intro.title: The Book of the Sea
intro.body: Pirî Reis compiled his Kitab-i Bahriye, a 130-chapter portolan of the Mediterranean, between 1521 and 1524 while serving in the Ottoman fleet. The surviving second edition adds notes from his own voyages along every coast he describes.

stops.name: Gallipoli
stops.note: Ottoman naval base. Pirî's base during his service under his uncle Kemal Reis.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Dardanelles arsenal with galleys drawn up on slipways, dockyard scribes at work on charts.

stops.name: Rhodes
stops.note: Conquered by Suleiman 1522. Pirî served in the Ottoman fleet at the siege.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Walled Hospitaller city under siege, Ottoman galleys blocking the harbour mouth.

stops.name: Alexandria
stops.note: Pirî gave the first manuscript of the Kitab-i Bahriye to Sultan Suleiman here in 1524 during the fleet's Egypt mission.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Egyptian harbour with Ottoman flagship, presentation of a leather-bound book to a turbaned sultan at the gangway.

stops.name: Tunis
stops.note: Mediterranean survey leg, c. 1522. Pirî describes the harbour and the Khayr ad-Din corsair base at La Goulette.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. North African harbour with corsair galleys at the mole, white kasbah on a hill behind.

stops.name: Venice
stops.note: Western limit of the Kitab-i Bahriye charts. Pirî describes the lagoons in detail though Ottoman ships could not enter without truce.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Venetian lagoon from offshore, Doge's palace and campanile, gondolas in the foreground.
